2606.23346.title: Field-level weak lensing cosmology with $<100$ simulations using multifidelity simulation-based inference
2606.23346.paper_summary: We perform a realistic KiDS-Legacy mock analysis with field-level neural compression and simulation-based inference using fewer than 100 $N$-body simulations. The weak lensing shear field encodes substantially more cosmological information than standard two-point summary statistics such as the power spectrum. Field-level inference can fully exploit this information, but physical realism at the field-level requires very high-fidelity simulations. This poses a major challenge for simulation-based inference (SBI): accurate empirical density modelling and deep-learning-based neural compression require many training simulations, but achieving physical realism at the field level makes each simulation extremely costly. We demonstrate that multifidelity SBI can alleviate this tension by substantially reducing the number of high-fidelity simulations needed for accurate cosmological inference. We pre-train neural inference models on realistic KiDS-Legacy-like shear mocks using fast log-normal GLASS simulations and fine-tune them on a small set of high-fidelity $N$-body simulations. We show that between $60$-$100$ high-fidelity simulations are sufficient to obtain informative and well-calibrated cosmological posteriors, enabling an order-of-magnitude reduction in simulation cost for accurate field-level inference in a realistic setting.
2606.23346.paper_summary_zh: 我們進行了一個現實的 KiDS-Legacy 模擬分析，使用少於 100 個 $N$-body 模擬進行場級神經壓縮和基於模擬的推斷。弱透鏡剪切場編碼了比標準的二點總結統計（如功率譜）更多的宇宙學信息。場級推斷可以充分利用這些信息，但在場級上實現物理現實需要非常高保真的模擬。這對於基於模擬的推斷（SBI）構成了重大挑戰：準確的實證密度建模和基於深度學習的神經壓縮需要許多訓練模擬，但在場級上實現物理現實使得每個模擬的成本極高。我們展示了多保真度 SBI 可以通過大幅減少準確宇宙學推斷所需的高保真模擬數量來緩解這一緊張局勢。我們在現實的 KiDS-Legacy 類似剪切模擬上預訓練神經推斷模型，使用快速的對數正態 GLASS 模擬，並在一小組高保真的 $N$-body 模擬上進行微調。我們顯示出 60 到 100 個高保真模擬足以獲得信息豐富且經過良好校準的宇宙學後驗，從而在現實環境中實現準確的場級推斷時，模擬成本降低了數量級。
2606.23346.author: Alex A. Saoulis et.al.
2606.23346.authors: Alex A. Saoulis, Kiyam Lin, Niall Jeffrey, Maximilian von Wietersheim-Kramsta, Davide Piras, Alessio Spurio Mancini, Ana M. G. Ferreira, Benjamin Joachimi
